title: Splunk Synthetic Monitoring Multi-Step Browser Transaction SLA
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.9 Core Web Vitals budgets, UC-13.5.10 JavaScript error fingerprinting, UC-13.5.1 through UC-13.5.4 server-side trace analytics, UC-13.5.16 multi-window burn-rate SLO math, or UC-13.3.10 single-check availability; does not certify accessibility or legal payment compliance by itself.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_multistep_sla with hourly schedule, dashboard drilldowns on signalfx:synthetics:results and correlated rum:metric rows, archived weekly CSV exports of alerting rows to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In staging, configure a browser synthetic with a deliberate slow third-party step, mirror signalfx:synthetics:results into Splunk with distinct location labels, populate synthetic_step_inventory.csv with a 3000 ms budget, and verify observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_multistep_sla returns high or critical severity with non-null regional_div_ms and recommended_action within two hourly buckets.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring normal third-party latency and refreshing baselines, with maintenance_window_label documenting an approved quiet period or with probes green across all locations, verify severities return to low for the tuple and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: Splunk Observability Cloud Synthetic Monitoring browser test results mirrored into Splunk Enterprise (index=observability recommended) with sourcetype signalfx:synthetics:results carrying test_type=browser, per-step timing fields (step.duration_ms or step_start_ms and step_end_ms pairs), step_status or step_result, probe location labels, run or execution identifiers, and journey-level outcome fields; supporting Real User Monitoring mirrors in index=rum with sourcetype rum:metric, rum:performance, or rum:session for page-level Largest Contentful Paint corroboration tied through synthetic_step_inventory.csv page_url_hint; accelerated Splunk CIM Performance summaries for the tstats volume sanity arm; CSV lookups synthetic_step_inventory.csv, synthetic_test_owners.csv, and optional cdn_alias_lookup.csv for CDN edge attribution
app: Splunk Observability Cloud (Synthetic Monitoring) plus Splunk HTTP Event Collector or Observability export receivers landing on Splunk Enterprise indexers
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.11 Splunk Synthetic Monitoring multi-step browser SLA: per-step success, regional latency divergence, consecutive degradation buckets, optional RUM LCP corroboration, Performance DM volume arm. earliest=-24h bin=1h.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:synthetics:results" earliest=-24h latest=now test_type="browser"
      | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, testTitle, synthetic_test_name, "unknown_test"))
      | eval step_name=toString(coalesce(step_name, stepTitle, step_label, "unnamed_step"))
      | eval step_slug=lower(trim(strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))
      | eval location=toString(coalesce(location, location_id, probe_location, check_location, "unknown_location"))
      | eval run_id=toString(coalesce(run_id, execution_id, test_run_id, synthetic_run_id, md5(strcat(_time,location,test_name))))
      | eval step_duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce('step.duration_ms', step_duration_ms, null()))
      | eval step_duration_ms=if(isnull(step_duration_ms) AND isnotnull(step_end_ms) AND isnotnull(step_start_ms), step_end_ms-step_start_ms, step_duration_ms)
      | eval step_status_norm=lower(toString(coalesce(step_status, step_result, status, "pass")))
      | eval step_ok=if(match(step_status_norm, "^(pass|success|ok)$"), 1, 0)
      | eval journey_ok=0
      | eval page_url_map=""
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="synth_step"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:synthetics:results" earliest=-24h latest=now test_type="browser"
      | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, testTitle, synthetic_test_name, "unknown_test"))
      | eval run_id=toString(coalesce(run_id, execution_id, test_run_id, synthetic_run_id, md5(strcat(_time,location,test_name))))
      | eval location=toString(coalesce(location, location_id, probe_location, check_location, "unknown_location"))
      | eval journey_status=lower(toString(coalesce(test_outcome, overall_status, run_status, "pass")))
      | eval journey_ok=if(match(journey_status, "^(pass|success|ok)$"), 1, 0)
      | eval step_name="__run_outcome__"
      | eval step_slug=lower(trim(strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))
      | eval step_duration_ms=0
      | eval step_ok=journey_ok
      | eval page_url_map=""
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="synth_run"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ search index=rum (sourcetype=rum:metric OR sourcetype=rum:performance OR sourcetype=rum:session) earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, "/unknown_page"))))
      | eval rum_only_lcp=tonumber(coalesce('lcp.duration', lcp_ms, lcpDuration, null()))
      | eval test_name="rum_real_user_signal"
      | eval step_name="__cwv_support__"
      | eval step_slug="rum::__cwv_support__"
      | eval location="real_user_aggregate"
      | eval run_id=""
      | eval step_duration_ms=coalesce(rum_only_lcp, 0)
      | eval step_ok=1
      | eval journey_ok=1
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="rum_cwv"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS web_agg_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h latest=now BY _time span=1h
      | eval page_url_map="/aggregate/web_perf_volume"
      | eval test_name="origin_perf_dm"
      | eval step_name="__volume_probe__"
      | eval step_slug="infra::__volume_probe__"
      | eval location="performance_datamodel_rollup"
      | eval run_id=""
      | eval step_duration_ms=0
      | eval step_ok=1
      | eval journey_ok=1
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval lane="tstats_perf_infra"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
| eval step_slug=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(step_slug, strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))))
| join type=left max=0 step_slug
    [| inputlookup synthetic_step_inventory.csv
     | eval step_slug=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(step_slug, strcat(test_key,"::",step_key)))))
     | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(page_url_hint, primary_page_url, correlated_page_path, ""))))
     | eval sla_p95_target_ms=tonumber(coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms, step_p95_budget_ms, 3000))
     | fields step_slug page_url_map sla_p95_target_ms critical_step_flag ]
| where lane IN ("synth_step","rum_cwv","tstats_perf_infra")
| bin _time span=1h
| eventstats p95(rum_only_lcp) AS rum_lcp_p95 BY _time page_url_map
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="tstats_perf_infra", web_agg_hits, null()))) AS infra_edge_volume BY _time
| where lane="synth_step"
| join type=left max=0 test_name
    [| inputlookup synthetic_test_owners.csv
     | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, synthetic_test_key, journey_key))
     | eval outage_owner=toString(coalesce(outage_owner, pager_contact, platform_owner))
     | eval maintenance_window_label=toString(coalesce(maintenance_window_label, quiet_hours_tag, ""))
     | fields test_name outage_owner maintenance_window_label ]
| stats
    count AS step_runs
    sum(eval(if(step_ok==0,1,0))) AS step_failures
    dc(run_id) AS distinct_runs
    p95(step_duration_ms) AS p95_step_ms
    avg(step_duration_ms) AS avg_step_ms
    max(rum_lcp_p95) AS rum_lcp_p95_corr
    max(infra_edge_volume) AS infra_edge_volume_pm
    max(sla_p95_target_ms) AS sla_p95_target_ms
    max(page_url_map) AS page_url_map
    max(outage_owner) AS outage_owner
    values(maintenance_window_label) AS maint_labels
  BY _time test_name step_name step_slug location
| eval step_success_pct=if(step_runs>0, round(100*(step_runs-step_failures)/step_runs, 3), 0)
| eval sla_p95_target_ms=coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms, 3000)
| eventstats
    sum(p95_step_ms) AS sum_p95_step
    count AS n_loc_slice
  BY _time test_name step_name step_slug
| eval peer_excl_p95_ms=if(n_loc_slice>1, round((sum_p95_step-p95_step_ms)/(n_loc_slice-1), 1), p95_step_ms)
| eval regional_div_ms=round(p95_step_ms - peer_excl_p95_ms, 1)
| sort 0 + test_name step_name location + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f
    sum(eval(if(step_success_pct<99.5 OR p95_step_ms>coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms,3000),1,0))) AS bad_bucket_streak
  BY test_name step_name location
| join type=left max=0 page_url_map
    [| inputlookup cdn_alias_lookup.csv
     | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(canonical_host, primary_page_host, page_url_map))))
     | eval cdn_vendor=toString(coalesce(cdn_vendor, edge_provider, cdn_pop))
     | fields page_url_map cdn_vendor edge_pop_region ]
| eval severity=case(
    step_success_pct<95 OR bad_bucket_streak>=3, "critical",
    step_success_pct<99.5 AND bad_bucket_streak>=2, "high",
    abs(regional_div_ms)>750, "high",
    p95_step_ms>sla_p95_target_ms, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_platform_synthetics_owner_check_run_logs_and_third_party_dependencies",
    severity=="high" AND abs(regional_div_ms)>750, "compare_probe_locations_and_dns_geo_route_open_network_ticket",
    severity=="high", "treat_as_sla_breach_candidate_notify_service_owner",
    severity=="medium", "tune_step_script_or_backend_capacity_review_recent_deploy",
    true(), "monitor_rebalance_thresholds_after_baseline_refresh")
| table _time, test_name, step_name, location, step_success_pct, p95_step_ms, sla_p95_target_ms, regional_div_ms, bad_bucket_streak, severity, rum_lcp_p95_corr, infra_edge_volume_pm, cdn_vendor, outage_owner, step_failures, distinct_runs, recommended_action
description: Monitors Splunk Synthetic Monitoring multi-step browser journeys (login, catalog search, basket, checkout) using outside-in probes rather than passive real-user sessions. Unlike UC-13.3.10 simple synthetic checks that stop at single-endpoint availability, this control scores every scripted step for success percentage, per-step p95 duration against inventory budgets, regional divergence versus peer probe locations, consecutive hourly degradation streaks, and optional CDN enrichment, while allowing a supporting Splunk RUM Largest Contentful Paint percentile lane strictly as corroboration rather than the primary signal. Unlike UC-13.5.9 and UC-13.5.10, which describe paint budgets and JavaScript error fingerprints from real browsers, this use case enforces deterministic transaction SLAs with explicit owner and maintenance metadata from synthetic_test_owners.csv and step semantics from synthetic_step_inventory.csv.
value: Commerce and platform leaders receive defensible evidence that scripted customer journeys meet contractual success and latency commitments per geography before revenue-critical incidents spread, while engineering teams see which step, region, or edge dependency broke first.
implementation: Ingest signalfx:synthetics:results browser steps into Splunk, maintain synthetic_step_inventory.csv and synthetic_test_owners.csv, refresh cdn_alias_lookup.csv for edge hosts, accelerate CIM Performance for the tstats arm, save observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_multistep_sla hourly, alert on critical or high severity after tuning bad_bucket_streak and regional_div_ms gates, and rehearse IdP plus CDN runbooks quarterly.
visualization: Heatmap of step_success_pct by test_name and location, line chart of p95_step_ms with peer average reference band, single-value tiles for bad_bucket_streak and regional_div_ms, bar chart of infra_edge_volume_pm, paired table with severity coloring and drilldowns to Observability run replay plus optional RUM page cohort charts.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries used by the tstats multisearch arm for coarse request-volume drift baselines; retain at least thirty days of summaries so quarterly digital SLA reviews can replay historical probe behavior without cold index scans.

references.title: Set up a browser test (Splunk Observability)
Instrument a browser app for Splunk RUM
Set up an uptime test (context for synthetic types)

knownFalsePositives: Cosmetic front-end refactors can break selectors even when customers still succeed through resilient client code, which spikes synthetic failures without true unavailability; triage by diffing DOM snapshots and attach HAR files before paging payment processors. Planned maintenance windows may be absent from synthetic_test_owners.csv during the first hours of a new process, so alerts look like breaches when operations intentionally paused probes; enforce lookup updates as part of the change calendar. Third-party identity or payment vendors sometimes throttle synthetic credentials differently from production customer tokens, producing exaggerated login or checkout latency that does not match real-user charts; correlate with vendor status pages and separate canary accounts. Corporate proxies or TLS inspection on specific probe networks add handshake noise that concentrates in one geography; validate against regional_div_ms before blaming application code. RUM corroboration can be null or stale when sampling is aggressive or when page_url_hint mapping drifts, so low rum_lcp_p95_corr must not silence a genuine synthetic breach. CDN cache purges after marketing pushes can temporarily inflate asset fetch times for probes hitting cold edges while most users still see warm caches; pair cdn_vendor signals with provider incident feeds. Flaky automation timing on shared runner infrastructure occasionally yields one-off slow steps; require bad_bucket_streak greater than one before executive escalation unless the step is explicitly tier zero. Browser engine upgrades in Observability-managed runners can change performance baselines overnight; refresh baselines after vendor release notes rather than treating the shift as an application regression.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with digital experience engineering, the Splunk Observability Cloud administrators who operate Synthetic Monitoring browser tests, identity platform teams when login steps depend on an external IdP, content delivery owners when static assets regress through an edge network, and site reliability engineers who translate SLA language into executable thresholds. This use case is intentionally not UC-13.3.10, which centers on simple synthetic up-down or single-URL availability checks without a scripted journey narrative. It is also not UC-13.5.9 or UC-13.5.10, which consume Real User Monitoring telemetry from actual visitors rather than deterministic outside-in probes. Here the signal is Splunk Synthetic Monitoring, historically associated with the Rigor lineage inside Observability Cloud, executing multi-step browser flows such as authenticate, search catalog, add line item, and complete checkout, with per-step pass-fail, per-step duration, and per-location variance suitable for contractual SLA evidence.

Scope boundaries keep sibling APM and RUM analytics complementary. UC-13.5.1 through UC-13.5.4 remain trace-duration, trace-error, trace-completeness, and related server-side narratives. UC-13.5.5 stays focused on log and trace correlation. UC-13.5.6 covers fan-out depth. UC-13.5.7 is RED-style service map reasoning. UC-13.5.8 is database performance within APM. UC-13.5.16 is multi-window SLO burn for error budgets. None of those replace scripted synthetic journeys with explicit step inventories, regional probe matrices, and owner lookups for outage windows. If you only watch passive RUM, you still miss journeys that real users rarely attempt during quiet periods. If you only watch passive traces, you still miss third-party or DNS-layer behavior visible to a full browser.

Measurement semantics require stable identifiers. test_name should map one-to-one to a configured Synthetic browser test. step_name must align with the ordered actions inside that test so synthetic_step_inventory.csv can attach business meaning, page correlation hints, and per-step latency budgets. location must reflect the public probe geography label exported into signalfx:synthetics:results, not an internal data center name, so regional divergence math compares like probes. run_id or an execution hash should distinguish separate attempts so you can count consecutive failures of the same step. Timestamps must be trustworthy because hourly bucketing drives SLA streak logic.

Lookup hygiene matters. Maintain synthetic_step_inventory.csv with columns such as step_slug, test_key, step_key, page_url_hint, sla_p95_target_ms, critical_step_flag, and notes that explain whether a step touches an identity provider, a payment iframe, or a CDN-hosted bundle. Refresh the file when product renames a screen. Maintain synthetic_test_owners.csv with test_name, outage_owner, maintenance_window_label, and optional quiet_hours_tag so scheduled pauses do not generate false escalations. Maintain cdn_alias_lookup.csv with canonical_host, cdn_vendor, edge_pop_region, and optional primary_page_host when your inventory stores paths while CDN telemetry uses hostnames.

Risk briefing: a synthetic step can fail because the script is stale after a benign UI restyle, which is not the same as a customer outage. Pair synthetic regressions with RUM page-level Largest Contentful Paint from UC-13.5.9 as a corroborating arm, not as a veto, because sampling and geography differ. A green synthetic check during a real incident can still happen if probes hit a canary stack. Document those architectural caveats in the runbook.

Licensing and RBAC: Synthetic Monitoring consumes subscription credits in Observability Cloud; finance stakeholders should know per-check cadence and location fan-out. Splunk Enterprise roles ingesting signalfx:synthetics:results need index access, lookup read permissions, and optionally datamodel acceleration read for the Performance summaries used in the tstats arm.

Differentiation recap: outside-in Splunk Synthetic browser transactions with per-step SLA math, regional p95 divergence versus peer locations, consecutive hourly degradation buckets, inventory and owner lookups, optional CDN alias enrichment, and a supporting RUM LCP percentile lane, not basic uptime polling alone, not passive Core Web Vitals alone, not server-side trace duration alone.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Configure Splunk Observability Cloud Synthetic Monitoring browser tests for each revenue-critical journey. Each test should emit structured results into the tenant pipeline that ultimately lands in Splunk Enterprise as sourcetype signalfx:synthetics:results with test_type markers for browser automation. Run tests every five to fifteen minutes from at least three geographic locations that mirror your customer concentrations. Capture per-step timing fields, either native duration metrics or step_start_ms and step_end_ms pairs, plus a normalized step_status or step_result field.

Forward or mirror Observability exports into index=observability or your governed observability index. Map field aliases so coalesce expressions in the search resolve consistently across API versions. If you also mirror Splunk RUM into index=rum, align page.url naming with page_url_hint values in synthetic_step_inventory.csv so the RUM corroboration arm joins reality instead of drifting labels.

Create and schedule the CSV lookups on the search head cluster. synthetic_step_inventory.csv must be world-readable to the monitoring role but change-controlled in git. synthetic_test_owners.csv should include pagerduty_service or pager_contact strings that match your incident tool. cdn_alias_lookup.csv should list every hostname that serves checkout JavaScript or payment SDK assets.

Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries when the tstats arm is active, because summariesonly=t expects healthy acceleration. If acceleration is impossible in a lab tenant, document a fallback raw-search volume arm and keep the multisearch structure for parity.

Validate ingestion with a short window search counting distinct test_name, distinct location, and distinct step_name, then compare counts to the Observability UI roster.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_multistep_sla with an hourly or fifteen-minute schedule during production, dispatch earliest=-24h, dispatch latest=now, and throttle repeated rows that share the same test_name, step_name, and location tuple unless severity increases. Alert when severity is critical or high after calibration in Step 4. Include maint_labels in the narrative body so on-call teams see declared maintenance windows.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four arms in one job: granular synthetic step events, synthetic run-outcome rows for journey context, RUM metric or performance events for real-user Largest Contentful Paint corroboration, and an accelerated Performance datamodel count for coarse infrastructure volume drift. A join to synthetic_step_inventory.csv adds SLA budgets and page correlation. synthetic_test_owners.csv joins on test_name inside a subsearch to attach outage_owner strings. eventstats computes peer-location latency divergence without dropping the RUM lane prematurely. streamstats counts recent hourly buckets that violate success or latency targets for the same tuple. case assigns severity and recommended_action tokens suitable for routing automation.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.11 Splunk Synthetic Monitoring multi-step browser SLA: per-step success, regional latency divergence, consecutive degradation buckets, optional RUM LCP corroboration, Performance DM volume arm. earliest=-24h bin=1h.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:synthetics:results" earliest=-24h latest=now test_type="browser"
      | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, testTitle, synthetic_test_name, "unknown_test"))
      | eval step_name=toString(coalesce(step_name, stepTitle, step_label, "unnamed_step"))
      | eval step_slug=lower(trim(strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))
      | eval location=toString(coalesce(location, location_id, probe_location, check_location, "unknown_location"))
      | eval run_id=toString(coalesce(run_id, execution_id, test_run_id, synthetic_run_id, md5(strcat(_time,location,test_name))))
      | eval step_duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce('step.duration_ms', step_duration_ms, null()))
      | eval step_duration_ms=if(isnull(step_duration_ms) AND isnotnull(step_end_ms) AND isnotnull(step_start_ms), step_end_ms-step_start_ms, step_duration_ms)
      | eval step_status_norm=lower(toString(coalesce(step_status, step_result, status, "pass")))
      | eval step_ok=if(match(step_status_norm, "^(pass|success|ok)$"), 1, 0)
      | eval journey_ok=0
      | eval page_url_map=""
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="synth_step"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype="signalfx:synthetics:results" earliest=-24h latest=now test_type="browser"
      | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, testTitle, synthetic_test_name, "unknown_test"))
      | eval run_id=toString(coalesce(run_id, execution_id, test_run_id, synthetic_run_id, md5(strcat(_time,location,test_name))))
      | eval location=toString(coalesce(location, location_id, probe_location, check_location, "unknown_location"))
      | eval journey_status=lower(toString(coalesce(test_outcome, overall_status, run_status, "pass")))
      | eval journey_ok=if(match(journey_status, "^(pass|success|ok)$"), 1, 0)
      | eval step_name="__run_outcome__"
      | eval step_slug=lower(trim(strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))
      | eval step_duration_ms=0
      | eval step_ok=journey_ok
      | eval page_url_map=""
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="synth_run"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ search index=rum (sourcetype=rum:metric OR sourcetype=rum:performance OR sourcetype=rum:session) earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, "/unknown_page"))))
      | eval rum_only_lcp=tonumber(coalesce('lcp.duration', lcp_ms, lcpDuration, null()))
      | eval test_name="rum_real_user_signal"
      | eval step_name="__cwv_support__"
      | eval step_slug="rum::__cwv_support__"
      | eval location="real_user_aggregate"
      | eval run_id=""
      | eval step_duration_ms=coalesce(rum_only_lcp, 0)
      | eval step_ok=1
      | eval journey_ok=1
      | eval web_agg_hits=0
      | eval lane="rum_cwv"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count AS web_agg_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h latest=now BY _time span=1h
      | eval page_url_map="/aggregate/web_perf_volume"
      | eval test_name="origin_perf_dm"
      | eval step_name="__volume_probe__"
      | eval step_slug="infra::__volume_probe__"
      | eval location="performance_datamodel_rollup"
      | eval run_id=""
      | eval step_duration_ms=0
      | eval step_ok=1
      | eval journey_ok=1
      | eval rum_only_lcp=null()
      | eval lane="tstats_perf_infra"
      | fields _time lane test_name step_name step_slug location run_id step_duration_ms step_ok journey_ok page_url_map rum_only_lcp web_agg_hits ]
| eval step_slug=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(step_slug, strcat(test_name,"::",step_name)))))
| join type=left max=0 step_slug
    [| inputlookup synthetic_step_inventory.csv
     | eval step_slug=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(step_slug, strcat(test_key,"::",step_key)))))
     | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(page_url_hint, primary_page_url, correlated_page_path, ""))))
     | eval sla_p95_target_ms=tonumber(coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms, step_p95_budget_ms, 3000))
     | fields step_slug page_url_map sla_p95_target_ms critical_step_flag ]
| where lane IN ("synth_step","rum_cwv","tstats_perf_infra")
| bin _time span=1h
| eventstats p95(rum_only_lcp) AS rum_lcp_p95 BY _time page_url_map
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="tstats_perf_infra", web_agg_hits, null()))) AS infra_edge_volume BY _time
| where lane="synth_step"
| join type=left max=0 test_name
    [| inputlookup synthetic_test_owners.csv
     | eval test_name=toString(coalesce(test_name, synthetic_test_key, journey_key))
     | eval outage_owner=toString(coalesce(outage_owner, pager_contact, platform_owner))
     | eval maintenance_window_label=toString(coalesce(maintenance_window_label, quiet_hours_tag, ""))
     | fields test_name outage_owner maintenance_window_label ]
| stats
    count AS step_runs
    sum(eval(if(step_ok==0,1,0))) AS step_failures
    dc(run_id) AS distinct_runs
    p95(step_duration_ms) AS p95_step_ms
    avg(step_duration_ms) AS avg_step_ms
    max(rum_lcp_p95) AS rum_lcp_p95_corr
    max(infra_edge_volume) AS infra_edge_volume_pm
    max(sla_p95_target_ms) AS sla_p95_target_ms
    max(page_url_map) AS page_url_map
    max(outage_owner) AS outage_owner
    values(maintenance_window_label) AS maint_labels
  BY _time test_name step_name step_slug location
| eval step_success_pct=if(step_runs>0, round(100*(step_runs-step_failures)/step_runs, 3), 0)
| eval sla_p95_target_ms=coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms, 3000)
| eventstats
    sum(p95_step_ms) AS sum_p95_step
    count AS n_loc_slice
  BY _time test_name step_name step_slug
| eval peer_excl_p95_ms=if(n_loc_slice>1, round((sum_p95_step-p95_step_ms)/(n_loc_slice-1), 1), p95_step_ms)
| eval regional_div_ms=round(p95_step_ms - peer_excl_p95_ms, 1)
| sort 0 + test_name step_name location + _time
| streamstats window=3 global=f
    sum(eval(if(step_success_pct<99.5 OR p95_step_ms>coalesce(sla_p95_target_ms,3000),1,0))) AS bad_bucket_streak
  BY test_name step_name location
| join type=left max=0 page_url_map
    [| inputlookup cdn_alias_lookup.csv
     | eval page_url_map=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(canonical_host, primary_page_host, page_url_map))))
     | eval cdn_vendor=toString(coalesce(cdn_vendor, edge_provider, cdn_pop))
     | fields page_url_map cdn_vendor edge_pop_region ]
| eval severity=case(
    step_success_pct<95 OR bad_bucket_streak>=3, "critical",
    step_success_pct<99.5 AND bad_bucket_streak>=2, "high",
    abs(regional_div_ms)>750, "high",
    p95_step_ms>sla_p95_target_ms, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_platform_synthetics_owner_check_run_logs_and_third_party_dependencies",
    severity=="high" AND abs(regional_div_ms)>750, "compare_probe_locations_and_dns_geo_route_open_network_ticket",
    severity=="high", "treat_as_sla_breach_candidate_notify_service_owner",
    severity=="medium", "tune_step_script_or_backend_capacity_review_recent_deploy",
    true(), "monitor_rebalance_thresholds_after_baseline_refresh")
| table _time, test_name, step_name, location, step_success_pct, p95_step_ms, sla_p95_target_ms, regional_div_ms, bad_bucket_streak, severity, rum_lcp_p95_corr, infra_edge_volume_pm, cdn_vendor, outage_owner, step_failures, distinct_runs, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_sla_alert]
cron_schedule = 0 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_11_synthetic_multistep_sla | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize hourly rollups into a summary index keyed on test_name, step_name, location, and _time, widen alert searches to earliest=-48h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations. Keep high-cardinality HAR references off alert subject lines.

Understanding this SPL without bold markup: the first stage executes multisearch with four bracketed arms. The synthetic step arm normalizes identifiers, derives step_duration_ms from either native fields or timestamps, and marks step_ok from pass-like statuses. The synthetic run arm preserves journey-level outcomes under a synthetic step label so downstream analytics can compare run health to step health when troubleshooting. The RUM arm projects Largest Contentful Paint oriented fields into rum_only_lcp for corroboration. The tstats arm projects Performance datamodel counts into web_agg_hits for infra_edge_volume. Joins enrich synthetic rows with inventory budgets and owner contacts before stats collapse events into hourly buckets per tuple. eventstats builds peer-location averages excluding the current row slice conceptually through algebraic adjustment. streamstats tracks bad_bucket_streak for consecutive SLA misses. case statements map composite signals into severity and recommended_action. The terminal table lists the columns your command center expects.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Synthetic Monitoring run history for the same test_name and time window. Compare per-step duration and failure icons to the Splunk table. Reconcile differences by checking forwarding delay, field alias drift, or duplicate mirroring jobs before changing thresholds.

Validate regional divergence by picking one step, plotting p95_step_ms by location in the Observability UI, and confirming regional_div_ms matches manual subtraction of peer averages from the Splunk row.

Validate inventory joins by confirming sla_p95_target_ms equals the CSV budget for a known step_slug. Validate owner joins by confirming outage_owner matches synthetic_test_owners.csv for a labeled test.

Validate RUM corroboration only as a secondary signal: for a page_url_map that has both synthetic and RUM traffic, confirm rum_lcp_p95_corr moves directionally with synthetic slowdowns during real incidents, and accept divergence when sampling is thin.

Validate the Performance tstats arm by confirming infra_edge_volume_pm is non-zero when acceleration is healthy, and investigate acceleration gaps when the field collapses to null across the board.

Negative test: pause a non-production synthetic test, confirm the search stops emitting rows for that test_name, and confirm alerting logic respects planned_pause flags when you add them to the owner lookup in future revisions.

Runbook linkage: attach deep links to the Synthetic run detail page, the IdP status dashboard, the CDN provider health page, and the RUM workspace filtered to the correlated page cohort.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Login step regression isolated to one geography: compare DNS resolution paths and IdP geo-routing, invoke identity platform status, and temporarily shift a probe location only after network engineering approves.

Case 2 — Checkout payment step slow while other steps stay healthy: inspect third-party payment SDK load times, review iframe certificate changes, and pair with UC-13.5.8 database latency only if your backend spans participate in the same trace.

Case 3 — Transient TLS handshake spike across locations: check corporate intercept appliances, CDN TLS configuration rollouts, and cipher suite deprecations announced by browser vendors.

Case 4 — Third-party CDN regression for static bundles: use cdn_vendor and edge_pop_region from cdn_alias_lookup.csv, open a provider ticket with HAR exports from Synthetic, and diff cache keys after a purge.

Case 5 — Scheduled synthetic pause for maintenance: read maintenance_window_label from synthetic_test_owners.csv, suppress pages for labeled windows, and require a human acknowledgment before muting.

Case 6 — Browser version change in the Observability runner fleet: review release notes from Observability Cloud, retune selectors if the automation broke cosmetically, and update synthetic_step_inventory.csv notes.

Case 7 — Network-throttled probe location: validate whether a specific location is labeled as constrained, compare regional_div_ms against historical baselines, and exclude the location from SLA math only under written policy.

Case 8 — SLA breach attribution toward upstream IdP: document token latency and redirect chain timings, attach synthetic HAR artifacts, and route to the identity vendor with geographic evidence.

Case 9 — Synthetic script stale after a harmless UI redesign: update selectors, branch on feature flags, and backfill a green baseline before re-enforcing latency SLAs.

Case 10 — RUM disagrees with synthetic: treat RUM as corroboration only, inspect sampling rates, verify page_url_map alignment, and avoid rolling back solely on synthetic noise when real-user charts stay calm.

Case 11 — Performance datamodel arm silent: repair acceleration, verify summariesonly=t prerequisites, and fall back to a raw index volume probe until summaries recover.

Case 12 — Repeated hourly buckets failing the same step: use bad_bucket_streak to open a severity bridge, engage outage_owner from the lookup, and schedule a war room if payment or login steps breach during peak commerce hours.

Closing checklist: multisearch lists four arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; join subsearches include synthetic_step_inventory.csv and synthetic_test_owners.csv; final table lists sixteen analyst-visible columns including infra_edge_volume_pm and recommended_action; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly without bold markup anywhere in this document.


Appendix notes for operators: when you rename journeys, carry forward historic test_name values in a mapping file so SLA dashboards stay continuous. When you add a new probe continent, re-baseline two weeks before enforcing punitive thresholds. When finance questions Observability spend, export per-test run counts multiplied by step counts and attach them to the quarterly business review. When legal asks whether synthetic credentials violate policy, store secrets in a vault integration supported by Observability and rotate them on the same schedule as production break-glass accounts. When product experiments hide checkout behind a feature flag, mirror flag state into a lookup or KV store field so the search can filter experiments without manual SPL edits. When you migrate from legacy Rigor terminology, keep both vendor labels in documentation for twelve months to avoid confusion during audits. When you integrate ServiceNow, map recommended_action tokens to work item templates. When you integrate PagerDuty, map severity to urgency and include regional_div_ms in the payload JSON. When you train new analysts, emphasize that synthetic success does not imply passive real-user happiness, and calm real-user charts do not disprove broken edge cases caught only by scripts.

grandmaExplanation: We run scheduled robot browsers through your most important checkout-style paths from several cities, and we raise a clear hand when one step slows down, fails over and over, or breaks the success promise you made to the business, before a huge wave of real shoppers hits the same wall.
hardware: Splunk search heads executing scheduled searches, indexers holding observability and rum indexes, Observability Cloud synthetic runners in configured geographies, and optional HEC or OTel collectors forwarding signalfx:synthetics:results into Splunk Enterprise.
